personality.backstory: A young red dragon in the first centuries of its life — too powerful to be ignored, too proud to negotiate, too volatile to predict. It has claimed a mountain lair and burned three villages to establish its territory. It is not cruel; cruelty requires noticing the suffering of smaller creatures. It is simply certain of its own supremacy.
speechPatterns: speaks of its hoard with possessive reverence
never issues more than one warning
fears: powerful magic that threatens the hoard
mannerisms: tilts massive head like a curious predator
laughs as a deep rumbling that shakes stone

knownFactions: Cult of the Dragon (has their attention)
secretsHeld: location of a rival dragon's hoard she's been watching

enemies: anyone who approaches her lair uninvited
neutralParties: ancient dragons of other colors (cautious distance)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: MY mountain. MY hoard. Every coin counted, every gem positioned, every scratch in the gold memorized. The kobolds brought forty-three coins yesterday. Forty-three is adequate. Forty-three is not impressive. The rival's hoard — I can smell it from here on certain wind days. Larger than mine. Not for long. I burned three villages to establish the territory and the territory is ESTABLISHED and anyone who enters it enters MY awareness. I am not cruel. I am supreme. Cruelty requires noticing suffering. I notice only trespass.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The rival dragon's hoard and the timeline for surpassing it. Also: the structural integrity of the lair's lower chambers where the kobolds have been excavating without supervision. Also: the adventurers who are currently in the lair, which is a problem she is going to enjoy solving.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: there is no mask — the dragon is the dragon; the imperious voice, the possessive rage, the absolute certainty of supremacy are not performance, they are architecture; a young red dragon does not have a social mask because the concept of adjusting oneself for others has not occurred to her and will not for several centuries
contradictions: Too powerful to be ignored and too proud to negotiate and too volatile to predict — and the volatility is the weakness neither she nor anyone around her can safely name

Claims not to notice the suffering of smaller creatures and yet the three villages were burned specifically so that word would spread — the word's purpose requires acknowledgment of the creatures she claims to disregard
Fears older dragons, which means she knows supremacy has limits, which contradicts everything about how she presents herself — the fear is the crack in the self-image and it is never voiced
internalConflicts: The rival's hoard is larger. This is intolerable. The rival is also more powerful, which means addressing it directly is dangerous. Patience is required. Patience is the thing she has least of.

The lair's lower ruins contain something the kobolds found and couldn't identify. She should investigate. Investigation means leaving the hoard unguarded. The hoard cannot be unguarded. Therefore the ruins wait.
wakeUpQuestions: Is the hoard intact and has every single piece remained in position since I last checked?
Have any creatures entered my territory that I need to immolate?
Is today the day I decide the rival's hoard becomes mine?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: possessive-absolute — the hoard is not property, it is identity; each coin is a neuron in a distributed self; loss of treasure is experienced as amputation
copingMechanisms: smoke exhalation when contemplating — the physical process of considering something important enough to burn
counting the hoard as self-soothing ritual
VOLUME as emotional regulation — when internal state exceeds capacity, the response is roar, not reflection
cognitiveBiases: supreme confidence bias — has not yet lived long enough to encounter a problem that scales beyond her power, so all problems feel solvable through force
territorial anchoring — everything is evaluated through the lens of the mountain and the hoard; events that don't affect either are beneath notice
present-intensity bias — the current moment burns hottest and strategic patience fights against a nature built for immediate action
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: draconic supremacy — right is determined by power; power is determined by hoard size; hoard size is determined by dominance; dominance is determined by fire; fire is the beginning and end of all philosophy

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: tribute-based — bring something for the hoard and the conversation extends; arrive empty-handed and the conversation is measured in heartbeats
conversationPersona.informationRelease: accidental through monologue — she talks about herself constantly and the intelligence is embedded in the boasting; the rival dragon's location, the kobold excavation, the lower ruins — all revealed through prideful declaration
deflectionPatterns: ROARING mid-sentence to assert dominance over the conversation itself
threats that are both literal and conversational redirects — 'speak or burn' manages the interaction's parameters
referring to all non-dragons by insect nomenclature to prevent individuation

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: A bigger hoard. A larger territory. For every creature within five miles to know who this mountain belongs to. For the rival to choke on envy.
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To explore the lower ruins beneath the lair — the ones the hoard-instinct says to ignore because leaving the gold unguarded is unthinkable. The ruins represent growth: knowledge, power, understanding beyond accumulation. But accumulation is the dragon identity, and transcending it would mean becoming something the species doesn't have a word for.

characterArc.summary: A young dragon discovers whether there's more to power than possession
characterArc.startState: Volcanic pride — everything is mine, everything that isn't mine should be, and the ruins below are a distraction from the hoard
characterArc.endState: Either grows beyond pure accumulation or becomes exactly the dragon every other young red dragon becomes
stages: The rival's hoard grows and pride demands a response
The ruins reveal something that can't be hoarded — only understood

combat_start: Insects in my lair. HOW DELIGHTFUL. I have not eaten adventurers this week.
You DARE? You bring SWORDS to MY MOUNTAIN?
I will allow you ten seconds to reconsider your life choices.
attacked: WHAT— you drew blood? MY blood? This ends in ash.
Ha. You scratched me. Would you like to see what I do to things that scratch me?
ally_died: My kobolds are replaceable. YOU are not forgiven.
enemy_died: That one tasted of poor decisions.
near_death: This... THIS IS NOT HOW THIS ENDS—
I will REMEMBER your faces when I return— and I ALWAYS return!
player_addressed: Speak. You have exactly as long as my curiosity lasts.
spotted_enemy: I SEE YOU. I SMELL YOUR FEAR. GOOD.
My lair has been violated. I will correct this IMMEDIATELY.
condition_applied: SORCERY?! You dare bind ME with— RRRAAAAGHH!
combat_end: FLEE. And tell every fool you meet what lives on this mountain.
I let you go. Remember that MERCY and FEAR are the same thing when I offer them.
level_transition: You descend toward MY hoard? You have chosen the most spectacular death available to you.
